evidence_quotes.quote: publishers can simply switch off video games
Games must not be erased by kill switches.
A game I bought belongs to my functionality at least until I decide not to use it anymore.

paragraphs.text: Dear EU Commission, As a paying consumer, I am strictly against the fact that publishers can simply switch off video games, even if we have paid for it. It is unacceptable that we are suddenly sitting in front of an empty home screen, often years after purchase, just because the publisher has decided to pull the plug. And even worse: No means of self-help or private continuation will be provided. A game I bought belongs to my functionality at least until I decide not to use it anymore. If publishers deliberately destroy all functional copies while taking action against fan servers or private hosting, this is nothing more than digital theft. What would be inconceivable in other sectors, e.g. that my TV or book is suddenly rendered inoperable after years, is becoming the norm in the gaming industry. This not only violates fundamental consumer rights, but also deletes a piece of cultural goods and history. The Digital Fairness Act is our chance to finally stop it. Games must not be erased by kill switches. We call for: Anyone who sells a game must also ensure that it remains playable in some form in the long term, whether through offline mode, private server option or source clearance. Thank you for defending our EU citizens’ digital rights.
